title: Revert "Revert "[InstCombine] Transform high latency, dependent FSQRT/FDIV into FMUL""

tests.source_program:
declare <4 x double> @llvm.sqrt.v4f64(<4 x double>)

define void @vector_sqrt_div_opt(<4 x double> %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz <4 x double> @llvm.sqrt.v4f64(<4 x double> %a)
  %div = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp <4 x double> <double 1.0, double 1.0, double 1.0, double 1.0>, %sqrt
  %mul = fmul reassoc <4 x double> %div, %div
  %div1 = fdiv reassoc <4 x double> %a, %sqrt
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare <4 x double> @llvm.sqrt.v4f64(<4 x double>)

define void @vector_sqrt_div_opt(<4 x double> %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz <4 x double> @llvm.sqrt.v4f64(<4 x double> %a)
  %fdiv = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp <4 x double> <double 1.0, double 1.0, double 1.0, double 1.0>, %a
  %fmul = fmul reassoc <4 x double> %fdiv, %sqrt
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary discusses scalar double operations extensively but does not explicitly mention vector type support. This test verifies the optimization works with SIMD vector types (<4 x double>), which requires the pattern matchers to handle vector constants and operations correctly.

alive2.src:
declare <4 x double> @llvm.sqrt.v4f64(<4 x double>)

define void @vector_sqrt_div_opt(<4 x double> %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz <4 x double> @llvm.sqrt.v4f64(<4 x double> %a)
  %div = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp <4 x double> <double 1.0, double 1.0, double 1.0, double 1.0>, %sqrt
  %mul = fmul reassoc <4 x double> %div, %div
  %div1 = fdiv reassoc <4 x double> %a, %sqrt
  ret void
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define void @vector_sqrt_div_opt(<4 x double> %a) {
entry:
  ret void
}
=>

entry:
  ret void
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
declare double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double)

define void @negative_numerator_case(double %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %a)
  %div = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp double -1.000000e+00, %sqrt
  %mul = fmul reassoc double %div, %div
  %div1 = fdiv reassoc double %a, %sqrt
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double)

define void @negative_numerator_case(double %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %a)
  %fdiv = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp double 1.000000e+00, %a
  %fmul = fmul reassoc double %fdiv, %sqrt
  %fneg = fneg double %fmul
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: While the code mentions matching -1.0 numerators, none of the existing tests demonstrate this negative numerator case. This test validates the fneg insertion path when the initial division uses -1.0 instead of 1.0.

tests.source_program:
declare double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double)

define void @multiple_r1_r2_pairs(double %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %a)
  %div = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp double 1.000000e+00, %sqrt
  %mul1 = fmul reassoc double %div, %div
  %mul2 = fmul reassoc double %div, %div
  %div1 = fdiv reassoc double %a, %sqrt
  %div2 = fdiv reassoc double %a, %sqrt
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double)

define void @multiple_r1_r2_pairs(double %a) {
entry:
  %sqrt = call reassoc nnan ninf nsz double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %a)
  %fdiv = fdiv reassoc ninf arcp double 1.000000e+00, %a
  %fmul = fmul reassoc double %fdiv, %sqrt
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary mentions collecting all matching R1 and R2 instructions using SmallPtrSet, but existing tests only show single R1/R2 pairs. This test verifies the optimization correctly handles and merges multiple R1 (mul) and R2 (div) instructions that all use the same sqrt/div pattern.

  %mul2 = fmul reassoc double %div, %div
  %div1 = fdiv reassoc double %a, %sqrt
